item.title: New austerity steps planned amid energy crisis
item.description: The government has planned to increase energy austerity further by introducing three-day online classes in educational institutions and by intensifying nationwide recovery of hoarded fuel oil on the back of continued sufferings of consumers in long queues at filling stations....

item.title: It’s Israel, not Iran, stupid!
item.description: FOR decades, western governments and media outlets have insisted that Iran is the gravest threat to global peace — a rogue nation, a sponsor of terrorism, a destabilising force whose very existence endangers the ‘rulesbased international order.’ This narrative has been repeated so relentlessly that it has hardened into conventional wisdom in Washington, Brussels and......

item.title: No admission test at primary level
item.description: THE decision to reintroduce admission examinations for school entry is unquestionably discriminatory. It is an education policy fundamentally misaligned with social reality. Inequality exists at many levels in society, but it is especially cruel in education, which is the foundation of a person’s future, self-respect, social mobility and civic potential. The opportunities......

item.title: Unsurfaced causes of death at sea
item.description: NEWS on the death of scores of Bangladeshi migrant workers by hunger and from diseases amid the perilous journey by rubber boats in the Mediterranean Sea, mostly starting off the coastline of Libya towards their dreamland, Italy, has somehow become the order, save the bereft families, who for some time wail loudly, drawing attention and sympathy of others, but......

item.title: Recovering from oil shock
item.description: BANGLADESH is facing an oil shock that is not of its own making, but how it responds will determine how deep the damage goes. The latest tensions around the Strait of Hormuz and the Bab el-Mandeb Strait have exposed a hard truth that Bangladesh’s energy system is too dependent, too narrow and too vulnerable. When these routes are disrupted, oil does not just become costly it becomes uncertain......

item.title: Unused Rajshahi hospital raises pressing questions
item.description: THE continued non-operation of a fully constructed government hospital for children in Rajshahi, about three years after its completion, is alarming especially as critically ill children continue to die amid acute shortage of intensive care facilities in the region. A recent report says that the 200-bed Rajshahi Government Shishu Hospital, built at a cost of Tk 35 crore on......

item.title: Minister’s denial can’t hide road safety crisis
item.description: ROAD safety in Bangladesh, among the worst in the world, has been the most pressing public concern. In 2019–June 2024, as the Road Safety Foundation says, 32,733 accidents took place across the country, resulting in 35,384 deaths and 53,196 injuries. The World Bank says that the increase in traffic accident deaths per capita in Bangladesh is roughly three times the......

item.title: Czech Republic beat Denmark on penalties to reach World Cup
item.description: Michal Sadilek's penalty sent the Czech Republic to the World Cup as they beat Denmark in a shootout in Prague on Tuesday following a 2-2 draw after extra time....

item.title: Italy to miss third straight World Cup after shoot-out defeat to Bosnia
item.description:
Italy will miss out on a third straight World Cup after falling to Bosnia and Hercegovina in Tuesday's breathless qualification play-off final, the Azzurri blowing their chance to reach this summer's tournament in North America with a 4-1 penalty shootout defeat.
...

item.title: Govt should widen tax net, overhaul subsidy: Debapriya
item.description: The government should expand the tax base, reduce exemptions and rationalise subsidies in the upcoming national budget as limited fiscal space and external pressures are constraining policy choices, said Debapriya Bhattacharya, convener of Citizen’s Platform for SDGs, Bangladesh on Tuesday......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: NBR considering raising tax for high earners
item.description: The National Board of Revenue is considering increasing the top income tax rate to 35 per cent for individuals earning over Tk 1 crore annually, as part of efforts to reduce income inequality from the 2027-28 financial year......

item.title: SME Foundation to provide loans worth Tk 125cr
item.description: SME Foundation, with the support from the Japan International Cooperation Agency, will provide loans worth Tk 125 crore at an interest rate of 9 per cent to cottage, micro, small and medium entrepreneurs in Bangladesh’s agro-based industries and food processing sector through banks and financial institutions......

item.title: Global players eye Bangladesh market
item.description: The 17th Asia Pharma Expo 2026 and Asia Lab Expo 2026 concluded in Dhaka on Tuesday, bringing together hundreds of global and local companies and highlighting Bangladesh’s growing importance as a hub for pharmaceutical manufacturing and investment......

item.title: Dhaka set to join textile expo in Frankfurt
item.description: In a resolute display of the nation’s burgeoning industrial prowess, three premier Bangladeshi firms are set to represent the country’s technical textile sector at Techtextil and Texprocess 2026 exhibitions in Frankfurt, Germany......

item.title: US gas price soars past $4 a gallon
item.description: The average price of gasoline at US pumps has soared past $4 a gallon, the American Automobile Association reported Tuesday, the highest for nearly four years on the back of the Iran war......

item.title: Asia to be hit hardest by Iran war energy crisis: Kpler
item.description: Asian nations are facing a major energy crisis as a result of the Iran war, with a sharp fall in crude shipments and few alternatives, global maritime analytics firm Kpler told AFP on Tuesday......

item.title: Contraband products threaten local shaving blade industry
item.description: The market for shaving blades is increasingly being dominated by illegal products......

item.title: Middle East war driving up shipping costs
item.description: Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz trade route in the Middle East war is driving up the costs of shipping fuel and goods around the world, industry data shows......

item.title: Oil prices keep climbing on Iran war uncertainty
item.description: Oil prices rose and stock markets diverged Tuesday as uncertainty over the direction of the Middle East war dominated investor sentiment......

item.title: USTR slams WTO after failed talks
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer on Monday lambasted the World Trade Organisation after high-level talks ended with a failure to extend a years-long ban on customs duties for e-commerce......

item.title: Indonesia rations fuel as prices soar
item.description: Indonesia on Tuesday announced fuel rations and mandated work from home for civil servants, as it seeks to conserve energy stocks amid global price hikes due to the Middle East war......

item.title: Mideast war pushes eurozone inflation higher
item.description: Eurozone inflation leapt in March because of surging energy prices caused by the Middle East war, official data showed Tuesday, hitting its highest level since January 2025......

item.title: South Africa cuts fuel tax as pump prices go up
item.description: South Africa on Tuesday lowered its fuel tax for a month to offset a global oil price surge driven by the Iran war, even as pump prices rose in one of the steepest increases on record......

item.title: Powell probe leaves US Fed leadership change in limbo
item.description: US president Donald Trump has repeatedly insulted and criticized Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell, famously calling him a ‘numbskull’ and demanding the independent central bank lower interest rates despite stubbornly high inflation......

item.title: Penguin Random House sues OpenAI over copyright
item.description: Publishing giant Penguin Random House announced a lawsuit against OpenAI on Tuesday, alleging that the AI-powered ChatGPT violated copyright by mimicking and reproducing content from a German children’s book series......

item.title: ICMAB, NBR seek to boost taxation, accounting
item.description: The Institute of Cost and Management Accountants of Bangladesh and the National Board of Revenue on Tuesday discussed enhancing accounting profession, strengthening taxation policies and fostering institutional collaboration between, said a press release......Education resources Bangladesh

item.title: Trust Bank opens Naogaon branch
item.description: Trust Bank PLC has recently opened its Naogaon branch located at Gour Plaza, Sadar Road in the city, said a press release......

item.title: Nepal arrests senior ex-official over protest crackdown
item.description: Nepali police arrested a former Kathmandu senior official on Tuesday, a spokesman said, the latest high-profile detention linked to a deadly crackdown on youth-led protests last year....

item.title: AB Bank signs deal with Scholastica School
item.description: AB Bank PLC has recently signed a memorandum of understanding with Scholastica School, said a press release......

item.title: Technical Education Division gets new secretary
item.description: The government on Tuesday promoted Md Daud Miah to the rank of secretary and appointed him as secretary of the Technical and Madrassah Education Division under the Ministry of Education....

item.title: ACC asked to seize tax files of ex-IDRA chair, wife
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Senior Special Judge on Tuesday ordered the Anti-Corruption Commission to seize tax files of M Mosharraf Hossain, former chairman of the Insurance Development and Regulatory Authority, and his wife Jannatul Mawa....

item.title: Myanmar junta chief elected vice-president
item.description: Myanmar’s junta chief Min Aung Hlaing was elected a vice-president by the lower house on Tuesday, parliament officials said, with the coup leader edging closer to becoming president and maintaining his rule....

item.title: Bank Asia receives IFC award
item.description: Bank Asia PLC has been honoured with ‘Best GTFP Issuing Bank in South Asia’ award by the International Finance Corporation in recognition of its excellence in facilitating international trade, said a press release......

item.title: Doctor jailed over fake medical certificate
item.description: A court in Sirajganj has ordered the imprisonment of an emergency department physician of Sirajganj General Hospital over allegations of issuing a fake ‘grievous injury’ medical certificate by altering a patient’s identity....

item.title: Modhumoti Bank observes Independence Day
item.description: Modhumoti Bank PLC paid tribute to the brave martyrs of the War of Independence on Thursday on the occasion of the Independence Day......

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 7 from human traffickers at Teknaf
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard rescued six women and a child from a residential house in Teknaf Monday afternoon....

item.title: Chhatra Dal at JnU gives 10-day ultimatum to vacate occupied halls
item.description: Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal at Jagannath University has issued a 10-day ultimatum, demanding the recovery of two student dormitories, Syed Nazrul Islam Hall and Abdur Rahman Hall, out of a total of eight student hostels reportedly under illegal occupation....

item.title: Sonali Bank Shariah supervisory body holds meeting
item.description: Sonali Bank PLC’s Shariah supervisory committee held its 30th meeting at the bank’s head office board room on Tuesday, said a press release. Committee chairman Zubair Mohammad Ehsanul Hoque presided over the meeting......

item.title: US envoy meets ICT minister Fakir Mahbub
item.description: US ambassador to Bangladesh Brent T Christensen paid a courtesy call on minister for posts and telecommunication, ICT division and science and technology Faqir Mahbub Anam at his secretariat office in Dhaka on Tuesday....

item.title: Idle Rajshahi children’s hospital to open partially within 3 months: health secretary
item.description: Health Services Division secretary Md Quamruzzaman Chowdhury on Tuesday said that outpatient services at the Rajshahi Government Shishu Hospital would be launched within the next three months, with full-scale operations expected in the next financial year....

item.title: Two DGMs of BKB honoured for excellence in sports
item.description: Under the initiative of the Ministry of Youth and Sports, a ceremony was held on Monday at the Prime Minister’s Office where a total of 129 national athletes were awarded special honours and financial rewards, including sports allowances, sports cards and recognition for international success......

item.title: Administrators appointed in 14 more Zila Parishads
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government on Tuesday appointed administrators in 14 more Zila Parishads in the country....

item.title: Power minister urges resolution of REB–Palli Bidyut issues
item.description: Power, energy and mineral resources minister Iqbal Hassan Mahmud on Monday directed officials to resolve ongoing issues between the Rural Electrification Board and Palli Bidyut Samities to ensure uninterrupted power supply in rural areas....

item.title: Sena Insurance approves 15pc cash dividend
item.description: Shareholders of Sena Insurance PLC approved 15 per cent cash dividend for the year ended December 31, 2025 as proposed by the company’s board of directors, said a press release......

item.title: DUET launches country’s first AI, Education Policy Lab
item.description: Dhaka University of Engineering and Technology on Tuesday inaugurated the country’s first Artificial Intelligence and Education Policy Lab, aiming to support research and policy development in the education sector....

item.title: Missing Schoolboy found dead after 2 days from Padma
item.description: The body of a schoolboy was recovered from Padma River at Sreenagar upazila in Munshiganj on Tuesday morning, two days after he went missing while bathing in the river with friends....

item.title: 2 UCB ex-directors sent to jail for embezzling Tk 25cr
item.description: A Chattogram court on Tuesday sent two former directors of the United Commercial Bank to jail after they surrendered in an Anti-Corruption Commission case involving embezzlement and money laundering of Tk 25 crore through hundi....

item.title: Govt adopts Health Nat’l Adaptation Plan
item.description: The government has adopted the Health National Adaptation Plan 2026–2031, a landmark step toward building a climate-resilient, inclusive, low-carbon and sustainable health system....

item.title: Unilever spins off food unit in multibillion dollar deal
item.description: Unilever said Tuesday it had agreed a multibillion-dollar deal with US spices maker McCormick & Company to spin off most of the British group’s food business......

item.title: Other countries should ‘just take’ Strait of Hormuz: Trump
item.description: US president Donald Trump said Tuesday the countries that have not joined the Middle East war but are struggling with fuel shortages should ‘go get your own oil’ in the Strait of Hormuz....

item.title: Microsoft plans over $1b cloud, AI investment in Thailand
item.description: Microsoft announced Tuesday it plans to invest more than $1 billion in cloud and AI data centre infrastructure and operations in Thailand over the next two years......

item.title: Tehran defiant, US-Israeli strikes hit key Iran sites
item.description: Iranian media reported Tuesday that a wave of US-Israeli strikes hit military bases, a religious site and a cancer drug plant in the more than month-old war rocking the Middle East and roiling the world economy....

item.title: Ex-DGFI chief Mamun placed on remand afresh
item.description: A metropolitan magistrate court in Dhaka on Tuesday allowed the police to take former director general of the Directorate General of Forces Intelligence, retired lieutenant general Sheikh Mamun Khaled, for five more days for interrogation in a case......

item.title: Newspaper owners seek tax relief
item.description: Leaders of the Newspaper Owners’ Association of Bangladesh on Tuesday sought tax and duty cuts for the country’s newspaper industry on the ground of mounting production costs and declining revenues....

item.title: Children exposed to preventable diseases
item.description: The closure of the operational plans-based health sector system without a proper alternative has disrupted immunisation and other essential healthcare services, exposing millions of children to more than 10 vaccine-preventable diseases, including......

item.title: BB RESERVE HEIST: BFIU for imaging laptop in BB custody
item.description: The Bangladesh Financial Intelligence Unit has sought approval from the Criminal Investigation Department for imaging a laptop which is in the Bangladesh Bank custody, following an order issued by the Supreme Court of the State of New York, NY County in the......

item.title: JULY CHARTER: Heated debate inconclusive, adjourned until today
item.description: The treasury bench on Tuesday proposed a special all-party parliamentary committee for amendment to constitution in line with the July National Charter, while the opposition called for a committee on its proposal for a Constitutional Reform Council session....

item.title: Iran fires missiles across Middle East as Trump threatens oil hub
item.description: Iran fired missiles across the Middle East on Tuesday as its capital was hit by fresh explosions, after United States president Donald Trump threatened the country’s key oil export hub, power stations and desalination plants......
